text.title: frontend-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for frontend-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tokens defined post-hoc after components already exist.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each component defines its own colors and spacing; extracting them later produces drift between Figma, CSS variables, and Storybook themes that compounds with every new component.

failure-mode.description: No a11y gate on design variants before human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents ship variants with poor color contrast (ratio < 4.5:1) and missing focus states. Catching these after selection wastes the refine phase on accessibility remediation instead of design improvement.

failure-mode.description: LLM generates JSX components with hardcoded hex colors instead of CSS variable or token references.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every color must change when the token changes, but hardcoded values silently diverge. Enforce with a stylelint custom rule that bans raw hex in component files.

failure-mode.description: Storybook story files generated by LLMs using outdated CSF2 format.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CSF2 stories fail or behave differently in Storybook 8. Pin CSF version explicitly in the prompt and add a CI check that verifies story imports compile.

failure-mode.description: Agent produces "5 variants" that are 5 minor color/font variations of the same layout.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a distinctness contract (navigation pattern, hero composition, information density), agents default to familiar minimal variations. This makes selection meaningless and wastes the brainstorm phase.

failure-mode.description: Reviewing variant quality from code alone without rendering to screenshot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM "looks good" reviews of JSX are unreliable; visual quality must be judged from rendered output. Always run Playwright screenshots before presenting options to the human.

failure-mode.description: Agent adds aria-label to already-accessible elements and omits it from icon-only buttons.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Misplaced aria-label pollutes the accessibility tree; missing labels on icon-only controls break screen readers. Run pa11y on every variant to catch both errors programmatically.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to "modern minimalist with gradient hero" — explicitly forbid clichés in the prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents have strong defaults learned from training data. Counter with explicit directives in the prompt about what NOT to do.

failure-mode.description: Variant rationales tend to all sound the same; ask for distinctness criteria upfront.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents need explicit axes of difference (navigation, hero, density) to diverge. Without them, variants blur together.

failure-mode.description: Agents copy Tailwind class soup without abstracting tokens — break out colors/spacing first, then components.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CSS abstractions must come before JSX. Generate tokens.json, then components that reference them.

failure-mode.description: Visual quality cannot be judged from code alone — always render to screenshot before shipping; LLM "looks good" reviews of code are unreliable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rendered output is the source of truth. Code review is a secondary check.

failure-mode.description: Agents producing JSX often hardcode hex colors instead of CSS vars / token references — add lint rule (stylelint, custom) to forbid raw colors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automated enforcement with CI blocks drift at commit time.

failure-mode.description: A11y: agents add aria-label on already-accessible elements (icon button has visible text) and skip it on icon-only buttons. Run pa11y on each variant.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Misplaced accessibility attributes are common. Programmatic audits catch both false positives and false negatives.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-loop checkpoint: variant selection — design choice is taste, not logic. Agent ranks but human picks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Preserve intentionality in design decisions. Agents can offer data-driven rankings; humans decide.

failure-mode.description: Component library scope creep: agents implement 30 components when you need 8. Maintain explicit components-needed.md list and refuse new ones.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce a whitelist. Every new component adds maintenance burden; agents do not feel this cost.
